next_steps: Microscopic examination of stool and tracheal aspirate for Strongyloides larvae
Skin biopsy of purpuric lesions for histology and tissue culture
Bronchoalveolar lavage with fungal and mycobacterial cultures
Perform bronchoalveolar lavage with fungal, mycobacterial, and bacterial cultures, including specialized stains for Strongyloides larvae
Perform punch biopsy of purpuric skin lesions for histopathology and tissue cultures
Initiate empiric ivermectin for potential Strongyloides hyperinfection
Extend incubation time for blood cultures to optimize detection of fastidious organisms like Capnocytophaga
Perform stool examination for ova and parasites
Send serum and urine for fungal antigen testing (Histoplasma, Blastomyces, Aspergillus, Cryptococcus)
Review serial peripheral blood counts for eosinophilia and perform a peripheral smear to rule out microangiopathic hemolysis
